title: Passenger Ferry Dock Closed
description: The ferry dock servicing the Cape Lookout Lighthouse area is closed for repairs. Visitors will have to use an alternative route, which includes walking about .5 mile on soft sand to access the boardwalk. This route takes about 15 to 20 minutes to walk. Visitors should wear sturdy, closed toe shoes because of potential hazards.

title: Hurricane Ridge Road Status
description: Hurricane Ridge Road is open. During the period of March 31 through April 30, Hurricane Ridge Road may open or close depending on road conditions, and weather. Check back here or call 360-565-3131 for updates.

title: Alcatraz Island temporarily closed April 25, 2026.
description: Access to the island remains temporarily closed for repairs to dock pilings. All scheduled tours have been refunded. To reschedule your visit, please call Alcatraz City Cruises at 415-981-7625

title: Alcatraz Island temporarily closed April 25, 2026.
description: Access to the island remains temporarily closed for repairs to dock pilings. All scheduled tours have been refunded. To reschedule your visit, please call Alcatraz City Cruises at 415-981-7625.

title: Temporary closure of some rock climbing routes to protect falcon nesting sites.
description: Northeast face climbing routes are closed to protect nesting falcons. Routes including and between The Cave and Kama Sutra are closed. The closure may be modified throughout the spring based on nesting behavior.

title: Malaquite Campground and dump station closed for road work through May 8
description: The Malaquite Campground and dump station will be temporarily closed until May 8, 2026, for a road resurfacing/paving project being conducted by the Federal Highway Administration. The campground will reopen for check-in (1:00pm) on May 8. Camping is available in the Bird Island Basin Campground.

title: BIB Boat Ramp & Parking Closed for Paving April 29 - May 3
description: Weather permitting, the Bird Island Basin Boat Ramp and Parking Area will be temporarily closed from April 29 - May 3, 2026. The closure is part of a resurfacing/paving project being conducted by the Federal Highway Administration.

title: Safety Alert: New Trail Open in Brandywine Valley Unit
description: A new multi-use trail is now open across from the Smith Bridge parking lot in the Brandywine Valley unit. The trail is open to hikers, bicyclists, and equestrians. A section of the trail includes a creek crossing that may be difficult for equestrians and mountain bikers. A bridge is planned for the future; however, visitors should use extreme caution when crossing this area.

title: Sheriff’s House Welcome Center Open April 25, 1:30–4:30 p.m.
description: The Sheriff's House Welcome Center will be open 1:30 p.m.-4:30 p.m. on Saturday, April 25th.

park_name: Great Sand Dunes National Park & Preserve
title: Air Compressor at Amphitheater Parking Area CLOSED for Winter Season
description: The air compressor and tire filling station at the Amphitheater Parking Area is CLOSED for the Winter Season. Vehicle operators using the Medano Pass Primitive 4WD Road should be prepared with their own portable air compressor before deflating tires in the area.

title: Anacostia Drive SE Temporary Road Closure: April 25, 2026
description: Anacostia Drive SE from South Capitol Street past the Anacostia Park Skating Pavilion in Anacostia Park will be closed to vehicular traffic from 5:30 am until 12:00 pm on April 25, 2026.

title: Cadillac Summit Road Closed for Construction
description: The Cadillac Summit Road is closed through April 30 for all uses, including pedestrians and cyclists, from the Park Loop Road to just prior to a parking lot at the summit. Hiking trails to Cadillac Summit remain open.

title: No Toilets or Drinking Water Monday, April 27 - Friday, May 5
description: Great Falls will be without running water from Monday, April 27 through Friday, May 5 due to a contractor replacing the sewage lift station pumps. Visitors should come prepared with water to drink and while the restrooms will be closed there will be portable toilets available.

title: 400 and 600 Loops at Alley Spring Campground Closed
description: Beginning Sunday 5/3/26 at 12:00 p.m., the 400 and 600 loops at the Alley Spring Campground will be closed for repaving. The closure will last through Friday 5/15/26, though sites may reopen sooner depending on weather and other factors that may influence project completion.

title: NPS 300 Road Conditions
description: NPS 300 to Big Tubes is dry and has been recently graded. It is passable for most vehicles.

title: All lava tube caves closed November 1 through April 30
description: All park lava tube caves are closed for the winter from November 1 through April 30 to protect hibernating bats.

title: El Calderon Trail closed for construction through July 2026
description: The El Calderon Trail and parking area are closed from August 11, 2025, through July 31, 2026, for a major improvement project. NPS Road 100 will remain open for local traffic only, and the Continental Divide Trail will remain open for CDT thru-hikers.

title: Trail Restrooms Closed
description: Restrooms located at the North intersection of Old Carriage Road Trail and Matildaville Trail are closed due a water leak. There are portable toilets available at this location.

title: Stanton Park Turf Area Closed for Statue Restoration
description: The center turf area at Stanton Park will be closed for restoration of the General Nathanael Greene statue through mid‑June. Thank you for your patience as we work to preserve this historic landmark.

title: Lassen National Park Highway under Seasonal Closure
description: The main park road (Hwy 89) is closed to through travel due to winter snow accumulation. Access to the Kohm Yah-mah-nee Visitor Center from the southwest entrance and Devastated Area from the northwest entrance at Manzanita Lake is open. Active spring road clearing operations are taking place. The road will not entirely reopen until late spring or early summer.

title: Early Closure - Mississippi River Visitor Center (4/24)
description: The Mississippi River Visitor Center will close at 3:45 p.m. instead of 5 p.m. on Friday, April 24th due to an event at the Science Museum of Minnesota.

title: Visitor Center Parking Lot Paving - Impacts/Delays Likely Throughout May
description: Repaving of the visitor center parking lot will take place over the month of May. Exact dates have not been determined but visitors traveling to the park during this time can expect impacts and delays. Check back here for updates.

park_name: San Antonio Missions National Historical Park
title: Parking Lot Pavement Work
description: Pavement preservation work is underway around the park parking lots and roads. You may encounter phased parking closures, temporary traffic control, and restricted access while crews complete crack sealing, surface treatments, and road reconstruction. Please follow posted signage during your visit.

title: Great Hall, Faneuil Hall Closures: April 2026
description: Due to scheduled events, the Great Hall will be closed on April 10, 12, 13, 18, 23, and 24. The Visitor Center Desk on the market floor will remain open.

title: Winter Seasonal Road Closures
description: The following roads are closed for the winter season or due to current snow conditions: Mineral King Rd, Crescent Meadow and Moro Rock Roads, and Panoramic Point Road.

title: Current East Rim Trail Status: Open
description: As of Thursday, April 23, 2026 the East Rim Trail System is open. For more information, visit the website linked below. In good conditions the mountain bike trails are open 6 am-11 pm for bikers and as posted for other trail users. In wet or hazardous conditions, the trail will close to all users to prevent damage.

title: Lava Tube Bridge Replacement
description: Most of Nāhuku lava tube trail will remain open during bridge replacement but will only be accessible one way in and out from the east “exit” end. The rainforest trail to the front of the lava tube, the lava tube bridge and 50 feet into the cave will be closed during the replacement. The bridge replacement should be complete by the end of June. The restrooms will remain open.

title: Chatham Closed for Rehabilitation Project
description: Chatham's interior will be closed until June 6, 2026, for a major rehabilitation project. The grounds remain open sunrise to sunset, and the gated parking area will be open from 9-5 daily.

title: Scotts Bluff Summit Road Delayed Opening
description: The Scotts Bluff Summit Road will open at noon on Saturday, April 25th due to the annual Spring up the Bluff running event.

title: Road Closures
description: The road between Castolon and Santa Elena Canyon may have mud and shallow water on the roadway.  Please slow down, drive with caution, and if there is any doubt about your vehicle’s ability to handle the conditions encountered, do not proceed!  Black Gap Road remains closed due to 2025 flood damage. Please check current conditions at a visitor center before traveling any dirt roads in the park.

title: Serpentine Hot Springs Bunkhouse closed for roof renovation
description: Four carpenters/laborers from Shishmaref are beginning a critical infrastructure project to replace the Serpentine Hot Springs bunkhouse roof. Work will begin immediately to address the issue as quickly as possible. The bunkhouse will be closed to the public starting Friday, April 24. The renovation is expected to last up to 14 days. We will update ASAP if the project is completed earlier. This important project is made possible through a partnership with Kawerak, Inc. Transportation Program.

title: Temporary Closure of Some Park Trails
description: Due to federal highways work set to take place, the park will be closing areas to the public. The Casa Rinconada Trail, South Mesa Trail, and the Chacoan Stairway overlook will all be closed starting on April 23, 2026. We don't know exactly when the work will be completed; hopefully in less than 2 weeks. Please call the visitor center for additional information: 505-786-7014.

title: Graves Creek Road Closed Until Repairs Complete
description: Graves Creek Road will reopen to vehicles after park road crews repair an eroded section at mile 4.1. Updates, including a construction timeline, will be posted as soon as they are known. To ensure equipment can move to and from the work site, vehicles may not park along the road during construction.

park_name: Delaware Water Gap National Recreation Area
title: Old Mine Road North Construction Closure Begins April 6, 2026
description: Old Mine Road between Jager Road and Route 206 will close to all traffic beginning on April 6, 2026. Contractors will work to rehabilitate a 3.5-mile stretch of road. The construction area will be closed to all traffic for the safety of park visitors and contractors.

title: Off Road Vehicle Inspections
description: ORV renewals & new permits will be issued at the Nathanial P. Reed Visitor Center Friday-Monday, 9am-1:30pm. No permits will be issued Tuesday-Thursday. Permits will be issued first-come first-served. Visit our ORV page for more information

title: ORV Permits Sold Online
description: Starting May 1st, 2026 ORV permits will be able to be purchased online. The physical office will remain open for the summer Friday-Monday from 9am-1:30pm. Please click the link to PREVIEW the site until it goes live on May 1st.

title: Infrastructure Improvements to Old Rag Trailhead
description: Effective May 4, Shenandoah National Park will begin infrastructure enhancements at the Old Rag Trailhead main parking lot. We expect to complete work by November 2026. Until then, the main parking lot is closed to all vehicle and pedestrian traffic. Visitors can access Old Rag Trailhead via existing overflow parking lots. Day-use tickets are still required from March 1 through November 30 in addition to a park entrance fee.

title: Shenandoah National Park Moved to Fully Cashless Fee Collection
description: On July 1, 2025, the park transitioned to a fully cashless system at our fee entrance stations. Only credit cards, debit cards, or mobile payments will be accepted.

title: Temporary Closure of Right Lane of Clara Barton Parkway
description: Temporary Closure of the Clara Barton Parkway Right Lane at Site 17, wooded areas between C&O Canal and Lock 10 parking lot, north to Lock 14, required to address ongoing safety concerns from DC Water emergency sanitary sewer overflow & bypass and pipe repair operations. Click on "More" below for specific details on the closure.

title: Ongoing Road Repair on Main Road
description: Ongoing road work will be taking place throughout the Main Road on the island. Expect potentially heavier vehicle traffic and areas of uneven terrain. Please yield to construction traffic. Be aware of approaching traffic both in front as well as from behind.

title: Restrooms and Water Fountains Unavailable at Akers Mill
description: Restrooms and water fountain at Sandy Point (Akers Mill, West Palisades) are closed until repairs are complete.

title: Lane closures on Piney Branch Parkway
description: In late March and early April, there will be temporary closures on Piney Branch Parkway for project work with DC Water and the Federal Highway Administration. As much as possible, these temporary closures will not occur during rush hour times.

park_name: Big South Fork National River & Recreation Area
title: Temporary Closure of Blue Heron Campground Sites 1-6
description: The following campsites at Blue Heron Campground 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and 6 are closed due to the close proximity of campsites to fruit-bearing trees that are used by black bears. This closure will restrict all campers from using the sites and is necessary for the safety of campers and visitors. For more information visit the alerts & conditions page.

title: Burn Ban in Effect
description: Due to exceptional drought conditions and extremely high fire danger, and in coordination with the countywide burn ban for all of Camden County, there is a burn ban for Cumberland Island. Please do not conduct any outdoor burning during this time as fires can easily become out of control and spread, rapidly endangering our island and community. This includes campfires in designated rings and cooking fires- no open flames. We thank you for your cooperation. Call 911 to report any wildfires.

title: Mainland Museum Closure
description: The Cumberland Island Museum will be closed Tuesday, April 21st, through at least Friday, April 24th for required maintenance work. We apologize for any inconvenience and will provide updates as they occur.

title: Historic Home Closed for Maintenance – April 28, 2026
description: The historic home will be closed to the public on Tuesday, April 28, 2026, for scheduled cleaning and maintenance. House tours will not be available. Grounds tours will continue to be offered at the following times: 9:00 am, 11:30 am, 12:15 pm, 2:00 pm, 3:00 pm, and 4:00 pm. All visitors should check in at the Visitor Center to reserve a grounds tour.

park_name: The White House and President's Park
title: Temporary Closure of Western Area of Lafayette Park During UK State Visit
description: The western half of Lafayette Park will be closed to the public to provide security and ensure public safety during the official visit of Their Majesties King Charles III and Queen Camilla of the United Kingdom from approximately 1:00 p.m., Monday, April 27 through approximately 4:00 p.m., Thursday, April 30, 2026. For a closure map and more detailed information, please follow the link below.

park_name: Home Of Franklin D Roosevelt National Historic Site
title: Special Use Permits - Application Charge Increase
description: On May 1, 2026, the charge for a special use permit application will increase to $125.

title: Special Use Permits - Application Charge Increase
description: On May 1, 2026, the charge for a special use permit application will increase to $125.

title: Special Use Permits - Application Charge Increase
description: On May 1, 2026, the charge for a special use permit application will increase to $125.

title: Temporary Closure for Marin Restroom Renovations April - June 2026
description: •Smith Road (Gerbode Trailhead) CLOSED 4/6 - 4/7 •Kirby Cove Restrooms CLOSED 4/8 - 4/22 (2 always open for campers) •Julian Road (Hawk Hill Roundabout) CLOSED 4/23 - 4/25 •Black Sands CLOSED 5/4 - 5/5 •Battery Alexander CLOSED 5/5 - 5/7 •Battery Mendell CLOSED 5/11 - 5/13 •Battery Wallace CLOSED 5/11 - 5/12 •Tennessee Valley Trail Head CLOSED 5/11 - 5/13 •Tennessee Valley Beach CLOSED 5/18 - 5/19 •Haypress Campground CLOSED 5/20-5/22 •Battery Spencer (Portables will be available) CLOSED 6/15 - 6/18

park_name: The White House and President's Park
title: Temporary Closure of Various Areas of the Park During UK State Visit
description: The entire Ellipse, including its side panels, roadways, and interior sidewalks; Sherman Park, to include the interior sidewalks; First Division Park, to include the interior sidewalks; and E Street, NW from 15th to 17th Streets, NW, to include the sidewalks along the above-mentioned park areas, will be closed to the public from approximately 6:00 a.m. to approximately 1:00 p.m. on Tuesday, April 28, 2026. For a closure map and more detailed information, please follow the link below.

title: Visitor center closed due to Typhoon Sinlaku.
description: The American Memorial Park Visitor Center is closed due to Typhoon Sinlaku. No estimate of when we will reopen. Most of the walking paths are blocked by fallen trees and other debris. Visitors should use extreme caution.

title: Paddling Conditions
description: Trees down across Cedar Creek between Bannister's Bridge and South Cedar Creek may require significant portages depending on water levels & paddlers should plan accordingly. All paddlers should have necessary equipment, including the 10 Essentials.

title: Kingsnake Trailhead Closed for Bridge Replacement
description: The Kingsnake Trailhead at South Cedar Creek Canoe Landing is closed temporarily as Bridge "L" is being replaced. The South Cedar Creek Canoe Landing parking lot remains open and accessible. Kingsnake Trail may still be accessed from the Harry Hampton Visitor Center entrance. Please see linked trail guide for reference.

title: Park Telephone System Problems
description: Big Bend National Park’s telephone system has had a malfunction in the voice mail system. Effective Monday, April 20, the park’s automated answering system and voicemail recording system will become inoperable. If you have an emergency, dial 911. If you know your party’s phone number, you should be able to dial directly, but you will not be able to leave a message. We apologize for the inconvenience and are working to resolve the problem.

title: Area and Trail Closures
description: Due to wind storm damage, the Lenape Trail is closed south of Mill Creek Trail and north of intersection with Raccoon Trail. The East Trail System parking area remains closed along with Civilian Conservation Corps (CCC) Pavilion. (Updated 4/20/26)

title: Janesville Segment — Partial Trail Closure
description: A 1.6-mile section of the trail from Riverside Park to Mineral Point Ave is closed due to flooding. To bypass this closure, use Parkside Dr, N Washington St, N Terrace St, and Mineral Point Ave for the 1.9-mi temporary detour.

title: Kolb Farm East Trail Temporarily Closed Starting April 23, 2026
description: Starting on April 23, 2026, the Kolb Farm East Trail (located in the southern section of the park) will be temporarily closed for the duration of a major trail construction project. The project is to replace the bridge over John Ward Creek that includes a major part of the trail. During the construction, pedestrian and equestrian travel on this trail will be impacted.

title: Seasonal road closures
description: The following roads are closed for the season: Heintooga Ridge Road, and Parson Branch Road.

title: Thomas Stone National Historic Site Closed for Winter
description: Thomas Stone National Historic Site will reopen April 25th, 2026.

title: Ramsey Prong Road and Ramsey Cascade Trail Closed
description: Ramsey Prong Road past Old Settlers Trailhead and Ramsey Cascades Trail are closed due to bear activity.

title: General Advisory — Flooding Possible
description: Recent heavy rainfall may cause flooding in various areas, and some roads may be closed. Heed all local warnings and signs. Stay alert. Remain on the trail. Use caution.

title: Select Roads Open to Regular Vehicles
description: Regular vehicles may now enter the park through the West and North Entrances to go to Mammoth Hot Springs, Old Faithful, Canyon, Lamar Valley, and Norris. All other park roads remain closed and will open throughout May, weather depending.

title: Hart Mine Road Closed
description: Hart Mine Road Closed.

title: Regular nightly closures of the Longmire gate have ended for the season.
description: The gate at Longmire to Paradise may still close for the night to support public safety depending on conditions. All vehicles, including 4WD and AWD, must carry tire chains and be ready to use them until May 1.

title: Carriage Roads open to pedestrians only
description: Starting April 17, the National Park Service will allow pedestrians to use the carriage roads. Bicycle use is prohibited until further notice to protect the roads during spring thaw, also known as “mud season.”

title: Pierce Stocking Scenic Drive Annual Closure - Open by May 22
description: Pierce Stocking Scenic Drive is closed to vehicles for the winter and spring season. The drive is expected to open by May 22, 2026. If you choose to hike the drive, stay alert for heavy equipment clearing trees and deep sand. Areas may be temporarily closed to hikers. Why is it closed? The drive is on an active dune. Winter storms and heavy snow can damage hundreds of trees. Strong winds blow deep sand atop walkways, parking lots, and the road itself.

title: Winter Road Closures
description: Wheeler Peak Scenic Drive is closed at the Mather Overlook. Snake Creek Road is closed for the season at the park boundary. Click "more" for current road and campground closures.

park_name: Craters Of The Moon National Monument & Preserve
title: Visitor Center Closure
description: The Robert Limbert Visitor Center will be closed April 30, 2026 due to a scheduled power outage. Please plan accordingly.

park_name: Black Canyon Of The Gunnison National Park
title: Long-term Closures Due to Wildfires
description: The following areas are closed until further notice: [1] South Rim Campground, [2] Rim Rock Trail from the Uplands Trail junction to the South Rim Campground.

park_name: John Fitzgerald Kennedy National Historic Site
title: Off Season Tours by Appointment Unavailable on Dates in April and May
description: Off season tours by appointment are not available on the following dates in April and May: April 6 - 10; April 14 - 16; May 15 - 28.

park_name: Fort McHenry National Monument and Historic Shrine
title: Building Closed for Preservation Maintenance
description: As the result of a comprehensive porch preservation effort, the Commanding Officers Quarters (immediately to the right upon entering the historic star fort) will be closed to visitors until work is complete. Please respect the boundaries created by construction fencing and do not enter the work site. All other exhibit spaces remain open for visitors to explore.

title: Upper and Lower Plaza Rehabilitation
description: The Plazas, Rotunda, and the Observation Deck of the Memorial will be inaccessible for the 2026 and 2027 seasons due to the Upper and Lower Plaza Rehabilitation. Thank you for your understanding.

title: Temporary Restriction on Campfires in the Backcountry in Effect
description: Due to drought conditions, there is a temporary restriction on campfires in the backcountry. Building, maintaining, attending, or using any campfire, ground fire, or cooking fire that uses wood or charcoal is prohibited. Use of backpacking stoves that utilize pre-packaged compressed gas canisters is still permitted. Carry extra water as springs may be running low or dry.

park_name: Big South Fork National River & Recreation Area
title: Temporary Restriction on Campfires in the Backcountry in Effect
description: Due to drought conditions, there is a temporary restriction on campfires in the backcountry. Building, maintaining, attending, or using any campfire, ground fire, or cooking fire that uses wood or charcoal is prohibited. Use of backpacking stoves that utilize pre-packaged compressed gas canisters is still permitted. Carry extra water as springs may be running low or dry.

title: 2026 - Double Arch Bridge Closed
description: The Double Arch Bridge near Nashville is closed for construction. There is a 7.5 mile detour from MP 437 to MP 440. Visitors can access the Northern Terminus to Big Tree Overlook (MP 440) as an out-and-back. The bridge will be closed to motor vehicles, pedestrians and cyclists until spring 2027.

park_name: Black Canyon Of The Gunnison National Park
title: Cashless Operations
description: Black Canyon's operations are now cashless. South Rim Campground is by reservation only via Recreation.gov. East Portal & North Rim campground sites are first-come first-served and accept payment using Scan & Pay through Recreation.gov's mobile app.

title: Historic Homes Closed for Season
description: The historic homes are closed for the winter season and will open May 1. Park grounds are open daily sunrise to sunset. The Visitor Center is open 11:00 am to 4:00 pm Mondays through Fridays. The Visitor Center is closed Saturdays and Sundays. The Visitor Center is closed on wintertime federal holidays, except for Presidents Day. From May 1 through October 31, the Visitor Center and historic homes will be open Wednesdays through Sundays.

title: Cascade River Road Current Closure
description: Cascade River Road is closed to vehicles at milepost 20 (Eldorado) two miles before the trailhead. Foot and bicycle traffic is permitted beyond the gate. Do not block gate.

title: State Route 20 Closed MP130
description: State Route 20 is closed at milepost 130 (Colonial Creek Campground) in both lanes. Diablo Lake Overlook is not accessible beyond the closure. For more information click the link to the WSDOT real time map.

park_name: Edgar Allan Poe National Historic Site
title: Site Closure for Utilities Improvement Project
description: The Edgar Allan Poe National Historic Site is closed for a utilities improvement project.

title: South End of Gravel Manahan Road Closed for Emergency Repairs
description: A portion of gravel Manahan Road is closed due to a collapsed culvert. The road is closed between Poplar Grove Youth Campground and Park Central Road from the south. The campground will remain open and accessible via Foxville-Deerfield Road onto Manahan Road from the north. No timeline has been established for the repair.

title: Big Oak Flat Road Fire Safety Work
description: To help restore more resilient forest conditions and safely reintroduce fire, Yosemite is conducting mechanical vegetation reduction work along Big Oak Flat Road between Foresta and Crane Flat. Crews are removing dead and fallen wood and reducing smaller trees near the road. This work reduces wildfire risk to visitors, infrastructure, and firefighters, and helps prepare the area for future prescribed fire or to better withstand unplanned wildfires.

title: Road Open To: Mile 13 (Mountain Vista)
description: The Denali Park Road is open to Mile 13, Mountain Vista. Wintry conditions beyond that point prevent vehicle travel, though pedestrian travel is permitted. Be prepared for winter driving conditions to reach Mile 13.

title: Wall Street side of Navajo Loop Closed
description: 4/13/2026 - The Wall St. side of Navajo Loop is expected to remain closed throughout this week due to hazardous conditions.

title: No Potable Water in Park Campgrounds
description: There currently is no water available in any campground in Great Basin National Park.  Water is available at the Lehman Caves Visitor Center and some local businesses.

title: Chickasaw NRA Road Preservation
description: Pavement Preservation Oklahoma and Pavement Preservation North Texas will be working in the areas of 12th street/ Bromide/ Rock Creek. Work is expected to begin on April 13, 2026, and last until approx. May 30, 2026. Construction activities will include asphalt patching, pavement repairs, striping, sweeping, or other roadway improvements. Work will generally occur Monday through Friday between the hours of 7:00 AM and 8:00 PM, weather permitting.

title: Johnson's Reef Buoys are OFF STATION
description: The aids to navigation around Johnson's Reef are currently off station. This means that the buoys that typically guide mariners through these waters are not in their expected locations. Mariners should exercise considerable caution when navigating the waters around Johnson's Reef. The absence or misplacement of navigational aids can lead to confusion, increased risk of grounding, and potential hazards. Use updated nautical charts and GPS systems to navigate carefully through the area.

title: Averill–Kelly Creek Wilderness Segment — Dangerous Ford at New Wood River
description: The New Wood River has risen and may be too high to safely ford. To bypass this section from the west, follow CTH‑E north for 1.9 miles, then continue east for 3.0 miles. At Burma Road, head south for 1.0 mile to rejoin the Ice Age Trail. Stay alert. Use caution.

title: A Few Small Roads Closed Due to Roadway Damage and/or Repair Work
description: These roads are closed due to roadway damage during past storm events and/or repairs to roadway damage: Middle Fork Rd, Redwood Canyon Rd, South Fork Rd, Crystal Cave Rd

title: Oversized Vehicles are Unable to Park in Visitor Center Parking Lot
description: Due to construction, oversized vehicles will not be allowed to park in the visitor center parking lot due to lack of space and ability to turn-around safely. There is oversized parking along the main park road.

title: Horse Trail Closed
description: The Horse Trail is closed due to saturated ground.

title: Andrew Johnson NHS Visitor Center Reopens
description: The park visitor center has reopened; Park information theater and sales areas are now open daily 9am to 4 pm.

title: Hemlock Creek Segment — Logging Activities
description: Logging operations along the Hemlock Creek Segment may mean heavy machinery and trucks in the area. The Ice Age Trail follows a logging road used for storage and transport of logs. The Trail is open. Stay alert. Remain on the trail. Use caution. Keep dogs leashed.

title: Indian Creek Segment — Machinery and Parking Closure
description: Recent logging near CTH-E (15th Street) may mean heavy machinery and log piles in the area. The parking lot on CTH-E remains closed to hikers. The Ice Age Trail is open. Stay alert. Remain on the trail. Use caution. Keep dogs leashed.

title: Straight River Segment — Partial Trail Closure
description: A 0.3‑mile section of the Straight River Segment south of 270th Avenue is closed indefinitely. To bypass this closure, from the eastern end of the Straight Lake Segment continue south on CTH‑I and east on STH‑48. This 2.4‑mile bypass replaces 2.0 miles of connecting route and Ice Age Trail.

title: Going-to-the-Sun Road Seasonal Closure
description: Going-to-the-Sun Road is closed for the season at Lake McDonald Lodge on the west side of the park. On the east side, the closure is located at Rising Sun. Travel on open sections of the road may change at any time due to winter weather conditions. Visitors should check road conditions before their arrival and be prepared for winter weather.

title: Park Closes at 4:30 PM May 12-20
description: The park will close nightly at 4:30 PM from May 12-20. All visitors must exit the park before 4:30 PM on these days. The park will reopen each morning by 7:30 AM, and return to normal hours (24 hours) on May 21.

title: Yaqui Ridge Trail Closure (Beginning April 13)
description: Due to nearby construction and blasting, the 1-mile Yaqui Ridge Trail will be closed starting April 13, 2026 until further notice to ensure visitor safety. The closure begins at the junction with Joe’s Canyon Trail, and access beyond this point is not permitted. This affects access to the southern terminus of the Arizona National Scenic Trail.

title: Cleetwood Cove Trail is CLOSED for Rehabilitation
description: The Cleetwood Cove Trail is CLOSED for construction. This critical project will fully rehabilitate the trail and marina to ensure safe access to the lake, provide a much-improved visitor experience, and protect the environment.

title: Monticello Segment — Stewart Tunnel Closed
description: The Stewart Tunnel is closed indefinitely pending reconstruction. The Trail is closed at both intersections of Tunnel Road. Use Tunnel Road to detour around the closure. Check with the DNR for details: dnr.wisconsin.gov/topic/parks/badger/tunnel.

title: Folger Park Closed for Rehabilitation
description: Folger Park will remain closed until June 30, 2026, at 11:59 pm. This temporary closure is necessary to provide for visitor safety during a comprehensive rehabilitation project to restore park resources and improve the visitor experience.

title: Southern portion of Drakes Beach closed to protect elephant seals.
description: The southern portion of Drakes Beach is closed until mid-June to protect molting elephant seals. The closure starts at the southern edge of the cove in front of the Kenneth C. Patrick Visitor Center and continues south towards Chimney Rock (to the right as one faces the ocean). The portion of Drakes Beach in front of the visitor center, and left towards Drakes Estero, is still accessible to the public. Please maintain a safe distance from all wildlife.

title: Protect Yourself from Volcanic Tephra
description: Volcanic ash, rock and glassy strands (tephra) is present at Kīlauea summit and can irritate your skin, eyes and respiratory system. For your safety, do not handle it. Eye protection, long sleeves, long pants, a hat, closed-toe shoes and a dust mask are recommended.

title: Kīlauea eruption
description: Volcanic eruptions can be hazardous. Stay out of closed areas and monitor air quality. Eruptions can last a few hours and are often separated by pauses lasting several weeks.

title: Ohanapecosh Campground area closed for construction in 2026.
description: The entire Ohanapecosh Campground area is CLOSED for the duration of the construction project, including all campsites, visitor center, restrooms, picnic area, & Ohanapecosh river access.

title: Nature Center and Planetarium Closure
description: The Nature Center and Planetarium building is closed until further notice due to continued remediation. Check the park's calendar for changes to ranger programs and locations.

title: Most Park Roads Remain Impassable
description: Most park roads, including Miners Castle Road, Chapel Road, Beaver Road, and H-58 between Grand Marais and Adam's Trail are not maintained and still have several feet of snow. Sand Point Road is the only park road maintained in winter.

title: Park Ski Trails Ungroomed
description: Ski trail grooming has concluded for the 2025/2026 winter season.

title: Great Allegheny Passage Erosion Near Confluence, Pennsylvania (Mile Marker 56)
description: Heavy rain & flash floods on June 8 eroded a section of trail along the Great Allegheny Passage between Rockwood and Confluence. Crews from Somerset County have placed caution tape for awareness. The GAP remains open at that location; please be cautious.

title: Trail Closure at Veterans Park at Balls Bluff (Leesburg, Virginia)
description: Veterans Park in Leesburg, Virginia, is closed for park improvements. All trails in the park are closed until June 2026.

title: The campground is closed for the season.
description: The campground is closed for the season. The campground will reopen for the season May 15.

park_name: Black Canyon Of The Gunnison National Park
title: Winter Closures
description: The following areas are still closed for the winter season: All North Rim facilities and vehicle access. North Rim Road is closed to vehicles and gated at the park boundary. Pedestrians and bicycles are currently allowed on closed roads, conditions permitting. Use caution and be aware of road maintenance equipment. Estimated reopening early May.

title: Tour Stop 7 Closed
description: Tour Stop 7 on the Battlefield Tour Road is closed for construction. Please follow closure signs and any instructions from staff or workers. Port-a-johns are available at Stop 6.

title: No dock access to Rainbow Bridge
description: At current lake levels, Rainbow Bridge docks do not reach the land. Dock-to-trail access to Rainbow Bridge will not be available until water levels rise again. Restrooms at the dock will remain open. This alert will stay up until the situation changes. Check the link for current lake information.

title: Park Loop Road Detour
description: A section of the Park Loop Road, from Kebo Street to Sieur de Monts, will be closed to all uses from April 13 to June 12, excluding Memorial Day Weekend. A detour will redirect motorists out to Mount Desert Street and Main Street following Route 3 to re-enter the Park Loop Road at Sieur de Monts.

title: Greenbush Segment — Logging Activities
description: A timber harvest is taking place on the Greenbush Segment. The work affects about 0.1 mile of Trail between Shelter #5 and the Greenbush Picnic Area. Heavy machinery may be in the area. The Ice Age Trail is open. Stay alert. Remain on the trail. Use caution. Keep dogs leashed.

title: Tisch Mills Segment — Seasonal Flooding
description: The Ice Age Trail from Mill Road to Nuclear Road is often flooded in spring or after heavy rain. To bypass this section, follow CTH-B between CTH-BB and Nuclear Road.

title: Roads to Juniper Lake and Warner Valley Closed for Repairs
description: Roads to Juniper Lake and Warner Valley areas are closed to vehicle access due to needed repairs. Roads leading to Juniper Lake and Warner Valley sustained significant damage outside of the park with no ETA for repair.

title: Clara Barton National Historic Site is currently closed
description: The Clara Barton National Historic Site is currently closed for safety related inspection and testing.

park_name: The White House and President's Park
title: Temporary Closure of Lafayette Park and Surrounding Areas
description: The National Park Service is temporarily and partially closing various portions of Lafayette Park and the White House Sidewalk on Pennsylvania Avenue N.W. for construction and turf renovation related to a major rehabilitation of Lafayette Park for America’s semiquincentennial. The closures will begin at approximately 5:00 a.m. on Monday, January 19, 2026 and continue through approximately 6:00 p.m. on Sunday, May 31, 2026. For a closure map and more detailed information, please follow the link below.

title: Temporary Closure for Muir Beach Restroom Renovations June 22 - July 7
description: Restrooms will be closed for renovation. Restrooms will be open on weekends and one toilet will always be accessible for visitors on weekdays. See map link below to see other restroom locations.

title: Perimeter Trail closed through June 30, 2026
description: The section of the perimeter trail from the 0.8 mile marker to the 1.9 mile marker at Park Central Road will remain closed until June 30, 2026, at 11:59 p.m.

title: South parking lot closed to vehicles through June 30, 2026
description: The south parking lot of Greenbelt Park will be closed to vehicle access until June 30, 2026, at 11:59 p.m. The area will remain available to pedestrians.

title: Temporary Closure for San Mateo Restroom Renovations May - June 2026
description: Restrooms will be closed for renovation. •Milagra Trailhead CLOSED 5/22 - 5/28 •Mori Point Trailhead CLOSED 6/1 - 6/4 •Sweeney Ridge CLOSED 6/8 - 6/11. See map link below to see other restroom locations.

park_name: Cane River Creole National Historical Park
title: Guided Tours Available by Appointment Only
description: To request a guided tour, please contact the park a minimum of two weeks in advance. The grounds and outbuildings of Oakland Plantation and Magnolia Plantation are open for self-guided tours from 9:00 am to 3:30 pm Wednesday through Sunday.

title: Hawk Creek Area Closed for 2026 Recreation Season
description: The Hawk Creek Campground and boat launch area will close to all public access starting April 16 through Oct. 6. The closure is necessary to keep visitors, staff, and construction crews safe during the Hawk Creek Road Improvements and Stabilization Project. It may be extended through Oct. 30, if needed. The project, managed by the Federal Highway Administration in partnership with Lincoln County and the National Park Service, will begin at the intersection of Hawk Creek Road and Miles Creston Road.

title: Tioga Road (Hwy 120 through the park) and Glacier Point Road are closed
description: Tioga Road (continuation of Highway 120 through the park), Glacier Point Road, and Mariposa Grove Road are closed for the season due to snow. Call 209/372-0200 (then 1, 1) to check road conditions.

title: Operating Hours
description: The seashore is currently operating on the hours of 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM. Last entry into the seashore is at 7:00 pm.

title: Road Construction Near the Park
description: There is road construction near the park visitor center along 111th St. and S. Cottage Avenue. Please use caution and allow extra travel time when visiting. Construction is expected to last through the fall. In addition, on occasion the vehicle entrance for parking at 610 E. 111th street is limited to one lane.

title: Lincoln Park Central Turf Panel Closed: April 4–July 3, 2026
description: The central turf panel at Lincoln Park will be closed from April 4–July 3, 2026, for restoration.

park_name: Jean Lafitte National Historical Park and Preserve
title: Barataria Visitor Center and Trails are Closed
description: Because of upcoming demolition and construction of the trails and Visitor Center, all trails at the Barataria Preserve and the Visitor Center are closed, with the exception of Twin Canals Trail This is a day-use area only with no restrooms. Please join us Wednesdays and Thursdays 10:00 am - 2:00 pm for programming and guided hikes along the Wetland Trace trail at 1:00 pm at the temporary Town Hall (at the Lafitte Barataria Museum location) and Wetland Trace, 4917 City Park Drive, Jean Lafitte, 70067.

title: Haleakalā Visitor Center Closed 2/19 through the week of 5/17
description: Haleakalā Visitor Center (elevation 9,740 ft) will be temporarily closed for a building improvement project from February 19th through the week of May 17th. Sunrise operations will continue as normal and restrooms will remain open. Headquarters Visitor Center (elevation 7,000 ft) is open.

title: Dogs Prohibited on Grand Parade Trails from April 1 to September 15
description: The Grand Parade Trails are closed to dogs from April 1 to September 15 to protect breeding birds nesting in the meadows. Nesting songbirds see dogs as predators, which can stress the birds and lead to less productive or even failed nests.

title: Change in Hours for Summer Season
description: Due to the potential of excessive summer heat, starting Monday May 4, The Lower Cliff Dwellings trail will close at 12:00P.M. The visitor center & park will remain open until 4:00P.M. These hours will be in place until Saturday October 3.

title: Cave Tour Reservations
description: Advance cave tour reservations up until May 31st are available. June reservations will become available on May 1st. Reservations are strongly recommended. Reservations full? Choose a day to come to the park early in the day to purchase a same day cave tour ticket.

title: High Fire Danger and Fire Ban
description: Due to high fire danger throughout the park, a fire ban has been put into place. As a result, the lighting of campfires is not allowed at this time. Charcoal grills are permissible as long as the charcoal is attended during use.

title: Aid Box Rock Climbing Area Closed
description: The Aid Box rock climbing area is temporarily closed due to a recent rock topple.

title: Closures on Park Central Road
description: Catoctin Mountain Park will begin repaving work on Park Central Road on Monday, March 30. The work will include closures of sections of Park Central Road between the Visitor Center and the Hog Rock parking lot. The closures will be in place Monday through Friday. On weekends, the road will be fully open. Work is expected to continue for 4-6 weeks. The Visitor Center will remain open throughout the project.

title: Theodore Roosevelt National Park moves to cashless fee collection
description: Starting May 1, Theodore Roosevelt National Park will accept only credit cards, debit cards, or digital payments for entrance fees, campground fees, and Interagency Passes. Cash and check payments will no longer be accepted.

title: Administrative Closure of Areas due to Nesting Shorebird Season
description: Effective 4/2/26: The closure includes the following areas within the boundary of the National Seashore : * North End * All island interior beach habitat along the northern section of Assateague Island National Seashore (north of Assateague State Park) is closed to all entry between KM 0.5 - KM 9.5. * The island interior beach habitats between KM 21.8 - 22.8 and 23.5 - 24.5 of the MD OSV Zone are closed to all entry. This includes habitat from the existing OSV boundary west to the vegetated shrub line.

title: Annual/Seasonal Canyon Closures
description: The following are closed to entry through 8/31/2026 to protect sensitive resources: the heads of Fivemile Wash & Burro Wash descending east 2 miles; Shinob Canyon + all routes descending into Shinob (including Na-gah, Nighthawk, & Timpie); and Arch Nemesis Canyon, including the route exiting at Capitol Gorge + all direct tributary canyons.

title: Presidio Trust Trails Annual Coyote Pupping Season is April to October
description: To prevent dog-coyote conflicts, avoid seasonally closed sections of the Park Trail and the Bay Area Ridge Trail to dog walking. Explore open dog-friendly trails instead and remember to keep dogs on leash. Visit the Presidio Trust's website for more details.

title: Fort Spokane Museum and Visitor Center to Close for Repairs
description: The Fort Spokane Museum and Visitor Center in Lake Roosevelt National Recreation Area is temporarily closed for critical historic preservation repairs. Dating to 1880, the landmark building originally served as the fort's guardhouse and now operates as the primary hub to introduce visitors to the park and its many resources.

title: SUP application fee increases to $150 beginning May 1, 2026
description: Steamtown National Historic Site will adjust its Special Use Permit (SUP) application fee from $50 to $150 effective May 1, 2026. This change reflects the administrative cost of processing and issuing permits and aligns with National Park Service cost recovery policy. This is not a new or expanded public-use fee. For questions, contact STEA_permits@nps.gov.

title: Island Ford Trail Closures Winter 2025-2026
description: All trails at the Island Ford Unit of CRNRA will be closed December 8 - 11. Crews will be felling hazardous trees; for visitor safety and project efficiency please stay off the trails. The Visitor Center remains open 9am - 5pm. Trails will be open until January 5. From January 5 through Spring 2026, Island Ford trails will be closed as we improve the trail system and add 2.2 miles of new trails. For more information, visit chattahoocheeparks.org/iftrails.

title: Beach fires temporarily banned due to dry conditions and statewide burn ban
description: Cape Hatteras National Seashore has temporarily banned beach fires due to abnormally dry conditions that may increase the risk of beach fires spreading into dune vegetation, forested areas, or developed neighborhoods adjacent to Seashore beaches. The N.C. Forest Service has also issued a ban on all open fires across the state.

park_name: John Day Fossil Beds National Monument
title: Blue Basin Overlook Trail Closed
description: The 3.25 - mile Blue Basin Overlook trail in the Sheep Rock unit is closed until further notice. A mudslide and trail washouts have created unsafe conditions for hiking. The shorter 1.3- mile Island In Time trail remains open.

park_name: Craters Of The Moon National Monument & Preserve
title: Loop Road OPEN; Caves Area Closed
description: The majority of the 7-mile Loop Road is OPEN to vehicles, with the exception of the short spur road to the Caves. Caves remain closed until June due to hazardous ice and snow. The Loop Road is also open to walking, hiking, wheelchairs. Please plan accordingly.

title: Conditions and Construction Affecting Cataract Canyon Takeouts
description: The primitive, dirt take-out at North Wash is undergoing construction this spring which might cause delays of up to 5 hours. Rafters are advised to use the foot trail past Highway 95 for passenger & gear drop-off (steep incline), continue on to Bullfrog, or plan their take-out when construction is not active: (Friday–Sunday or before 7am / after 6pm Monday–Thursday).

title: Conditions and Construction Affecting Cataract Canyon Takeouts
description: Hite Ramp is closed. The primitive, dirt take-out at North Wash is undergoing construction this spring which might cause delays of up to 5 hours. Boaters are advised to use the foot trail past Highway 95 for passenger & gear drop-off (steep incline), continue on to Bullfrog, or plan their take-out when construction is not active: (Friday–Sunday or before 7am / after 6pm Monday–Thursday).

title: Temporary Emergency Fire Restrictions
description: Due to dry conditions and high fire danger, open fires are prohibited in Wind Cave National Park until further notice. Gas stoves used in or on fire resistant containers are permitted only in the Elk Mountain Campground and the Picnic Area. No smoking is allowed except in vehicles or on paved or concrete areas next to the visitor center. Fires of any kind on trails or in backcountry areas are prohibited. This includes smoking, gas stoves and open fires.

title: Partial Closures to Marquette Greenway Trail and Glenwood Dunes Trail
description: Marquette Greenway construction will require closing sections of the Glenwood Dunes Trail (April–December 2026) and Little Calumet / Mnoké Prairie Trail (May–December 2026). Closure dates may shift as work progresses. Please follow all posted closures and check the park website or NPS App for updates.

title: Alava Trail Closure
description: The Mount Alava trail and Fatifati trail from the Fagasa pass to the summit is closed for trail improvement work until further notice. Visitors can hike to the Alava trail from the Old Vatia trail, it is a much steeper and more rugged climb. Other suggested hiking alternatives include Upper and Lower Sauma trails and the Tuafanua trail.

title: Launch Ramp Updates
description: Lake level changes will influence the functionality of all launch ramps. Find updated status of all launch ramps at the link.

park_name: Dayton Aviation Heritage National Historical Park
title: Phone System Issues and Updated Contact Number
description: Dayton Aviation Heritage is currently experiencing phone system issues. 937-225-7705 is not in service, and so a temporary number is in place. Please call 937-345-0433 for information on the park.

park_name: Bent's Old Fort National Historic Site
title: Beat the Heat!
description: Make sure to take weather into consideration when visiting. Carry water and use sunscreen to reduce the risk of heat injuries during your visit.

title: Photo Season
description: Just a reminder as the weather is starting to get nice, and people are thinking about where to take graduation, wedding, prom or formal, pregnancy, birthday, and any other type of photography or filming! We do have rules and regulations on what is or is not allowed and whether or not you need a permit. Violations may be ticketed. Please check our website under Plan Your Visit, Permits and Reservations!!! https://www.nps.gov/frhi/planyourvisit/permits.htm

title: Dead Horse Bay (Brooklyn) Remains Closed to the Public
description: The entire southern area of Dead Horse Bay, including Glass Bottle Beach, is closed to the public. Dead Horse Bay is located in Brooklyn, NYC.

title: Mount Baldy Closure
description: Closure: The Mount Baldy Access Site will be closed from late March through mid-June, 2026, for sand removal and shoreline stabilization work.

title: Gallatin House Hours Changing
description: The Gallatin House and Visitor Center will only be open Saturdays and Sundays until further notice, 9 AM to 5 PM. The grounds and trails will be available from sunrise to sunset year-round.

title: Stage 1 Burn Restrictions
description: Discharge of fireworks are prohibited. Only campfires at campsites within a fire ring are permitted. Charcoal fires within enclosed grills are permitted. Portable stoves are permitted.

park_name: Home Of Franklin D Roosevelt National Historic Site
title: Partial closure of the Rose Garden from March 31 until early summer
description: The planting beds, lawns, and paths of the Rose Garden will be under construction beginning in late March 2026. This rehabilitation will restore the crisp, level character of the garden, improve access and infrastructure, and re-install historic rose and peony varieties. During this period, the Burial Site viewing area should remain available, but occasional disruptions may restrict public access. The graves, marble monument, pet graves, and sundial will not be impacted by this project.

title: Temporary Museum Closure
description: The park museum in the visitor center is temporarily closed for the installation of new exhibits. All other areas within the visitor center such as the welcome desk, theater, and bookstore will remain open during the project. The museum will reopen in the Spring of 2026.

title: Trail update 3/23/26
description: Most trails are open and ready for the public. Those that remain closed are the Charleston Road/ Goudey Trail past the Cherokee Path and the portion of Cherokee Path between the stockade fort and the Charleston Road.

title: George Washington Inaugural Bible
description: The George Washington Inaugural Bible is currently offsite.

title: Sidewalk scaffolding at Federal Hall
description: Protective sidewalk scaffolding has been erected around the building to protect the public from falling materials.

title: Little Yellow Creek Bridge Closure - Construction Underway
description: Work to replace the Little Yellow Creek Bridge on Bartlett Park Road has begun. Bartlett Park remains open and accessible, but visitors will see and hear construction activities. The road and areas south of the bridge, including Marsee Cemetery, will not be accessible during construction.

title: Oregon Caves opens for the 2026 season
description: Oregon Caves will open March 21, 2026. Cave tours will be available Thursdays thru Mondays from 10:00 am to 4:00 pm and closed on Tuesdays and Wednesdays. Feel free to call 541-592-5125 for more information.

title: Oasis of Mara Trail Partial Closure
description: Due to a heavy flooding event, the Oasis of Mara Trail is closed past the Oasis.

title: Shuttle Service Begins March 19
description: Bandelier National Monument's free shuttle service to Frijoles Canyon begins on Thursday, March 19, 2026. The shuttle boards at the Frey Trail Parking Lot starting at 10:30 am. The shuttle is not mandatory unless the parking lots in the canyon are filled up. If all parking lots fill both in Frijoles Canyon and the Frey Trail, cars will then be turned around at the park entrance until more spaces become available. We appreciate your patience while navigating an influx in visitation.

title: Temporary Closure of Petroglyph Boardwalk
description: We will be working to replace the petroglyph boardwalk beginning on October 27th, 2025, through June 1st, 2026. During this time, the boardwalk will be closed for renovations.

title: Traffic Delays - Gardner River High Bridge
description: Expect a single lane of traffic with up to 15-minute delays, 24 hours a day, seven days a week, from April 1 through late October 2026. Oversized/overweight vehicles (8'6"+ wide, 75'+ long, 80,000 lbs+ gross vehicle weight) will not be able to cross the bridge during this time.

title: Blasting in Memorial
description: Blasting activity is occurring in the Coronado National Forest, Huachuca Mountains, and within Coronado National Memorial. Visitors may hear loud noise and notice dust in the air.

title: Spring 2026 Prescribed Fire Notice at Lake Roosevelt
description: Lake Roosevelt National Recreation Area may implement the following prescribed burn during the spring of 2026.

title: Trip-planning tips for 2026
description: Planning to visit Arches this year? To ensure the best experience, please anticipate potentially long wait times at the entrance station. If trailhead parking is full, plan to return a little later. Please avoid parking on vegetation and roadways to help preserve this beautiful place for everyone to enjoy.

title: Scam Warning - Book ferry tickets through concessioner site only
description: Valid ferry tickets can only be purchased through the website www.cumberlandislandferry.com. Check site carefully before purchasing. Only valid tickets will be honored.

title: Trip-planning tips for 2026
description: Planning to visit Canyonlands this year? To ensure the best experience, please anticipate potentially long wait times at the entrance station. If overlook or trailhead parking is full, plan to return a little later. Please avoid parking on vegetation and roadways to help preserve this beautiful place for everyone to enjoy.

title: Waterside Theatre Temporarily Closed
description: The Waterside Theatre is temporarily closed, due to a roofing project. Once the project is completed, the area will reopen.

title: Fort Pickens Campground Restroom A Loop Closure
description: The park is remodeling the interior of the restroom structure at loop A. There will portable restroom and shower trailers set up in this area. All other restrooms will remain open for campers with reservations during this time.

title: China Beach Construction Phase 2 Begins in July 2025
description: Project continues with improvements to the lower terrace and picnic areas, rehabbing the interior and exterior of the bathhouse, and installing seating and foot wash station near beach. Projects are expected to take 16-18 months. See link for details.

title: Phone Service Unavailable
description: Phone service at Puʻukoholā Heiau National Historic Site is currently unavailable. If you need to contact the park, please email us at puhe_info@nps.gov and a staff member will respond as soon as possible. We appreciate your patience while service is being restored.

title: OSV Status
description: 3/15/26 MD OSV Open to State Line, VA South OSV partially open. Closure begins at the old Coast Guard Station and extends to the hook. North OSV closed.

title: Telephone System is Experiencing Connectivity Issues
description: The phone system at Sagamore Hill is experiencing connectivity issues. Please contact the park via email at sahi_information@nps.gov when phones are out of service.

title: Bumpass Hell Trail Closed
description: The Bumpass Hell Trail is under a seasonal closure due to accumulated winter snow. This is an annual closure to prevent injury from treacherous trail conditions. The trail typically remains closed through mid-July. Learn more

title: Road Construction Begins
description: In late March, simultaneous road improvements begin along the northern end of the Park Road, and the access roads to Cedar Tree Tower and Far View sites in the south. Travel delays up to 30 minutes are possible. Click the link for the park map.

title: Pet Restrictions, Beach Driving Closures in Effect March 15 through Labor Day
description: To protect threatened nesting shorebirds, pets are prohibited in the Otis Pike Wilderness and on federally-managed ocean beaches. Beach driving is closed at Lighthouse Beach, Sailors Haven and the Wilderness area.

title: Avian Influenza (HPAI) Suspected in Dead Birds in Seashore Areas
description: Dead birds observed along beaches and marsh shorelines may have been infected with Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza (HPAI). The New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (NYSDEC) advises the public to avoid contact with sick or dead birds and mammals that may be infected with HPAI. Pets should be secured at all times and kept away from all wild animals. See the link below for more information.

title: North Parkway Rehabilitation
description: To get more information about the North Parkway rehabilitation, sign up for our newsletter.

title: Temporary Closures of Park Entrance Possible if Parking Lots Become Full
description: If parking lots become full, there will be a temporary closure of the park entrance at Georgetown Pike & Old Dominion Drive. The park entrance will re-open as soon as space becomes available. For updated information about parking, call Visitor Center at 703-757-3101

title: A harmful algal bloom (HAB) has been detected in Government Wash
description: Bright green algae (cyanobacteria) floating on the water may contain toxins and is unsafe for swimming or contact. Children and pets are at higher risk if contaminated water is swallowed. Keep pets leashed and away from the water. Seek medical or veterinary care if symptoms occur after exposure. Click More for photos of the harmful algae as well as symptoms in humans or pets if exposed to the bacteria.

title: Reds Meadow Road Access Restrictions in 2026
description: Reds Meadow Road remains under construction in 2026 and will be closed Mondays through Wednesdays from July 6 through September 20 and Monday through Friday starting September 21. There is no estimate for when the road will open for the season.

title: Devils Postpile National Monument facilities are closed for the season
description: Devils Postpile National Monument facilities are closed for season, with no public programs, parking, water, or bathroom facilities available.

title: Ocean Beach Fire Season in Effect through October 31, 2026
description: Fires allowed ONLY in park provided concrete fire rings between Stairwells 15 and 20. Do NOT make fire rings using rocks, logs or other items. For more information, visit the beach fire regulations link below.

title: Visitor Center Restrooms
description: The visitor center restrooms are currently being remodeled. Restrooms are available in a trailer just behind the restroom remodel.

title: 65th Anniversary Event
description: Freedom Riders National Monument will commemorate the 65th anniversary of the bus burning and attacks on Freedom Riders in Anniston, Alabama. This milestone event will commemorate those riders that were attacked here in Anniston, providing moments of reflection and education from park rangers and speakers. The event will take place on Saturday, May 9, 2026, the day before Mother’s Day. Go to the event page to learn more. Freedom Riders staff will update this page with updates closer to the event.

park_name: San Francisco Maritime National Historical Park
title: Aquatic Park Bathhouse Elevator Out of Service
description: The elevator in the Aquatic Park Bathhouse is currently out of service. Repairs will take roughly 3-4 weeks. The second and third floors may be used for access during operating hours.

title: Pothole Repairs Along the Parkway
description: The park is actively working to inspect road surfaces and repair potholes. To report a pothole or initiate a tort claim for personal property damage associated with a pothole, please e-mail GWMP_Superintendent@nps.gov.

title: Parking Lot E Closed for Construction
description: Parking Lot E along the park's Scenic Drive is closed for construction. The park is currently working to expand this parking area, and the lot will be closed while the expansion is being built. Visitors can still access the Mary Byrd Branch Trail and Quantico Cascades Trail by following trail reroute signs and parking vehicles in Turkey Run parking area. For your safety, please follow all closures and only park in designated parking spots.

title: Mammoth Hot Springs - Palette Springs Boardwalk Closure
description: A portion of the boardwalk leading to Palette Springs, located on the Lower Terraces in Mammoth Hot Springs, will be temporarily closed for repair work.

title: Spring Hours
description: Visitor Access to Fort Donelson NB Park Lands: Pedestrian Access is from sunrise to sunset. Automobile Access to Fort Donelson NB Park Roads 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM daily.

title: Seasonal Closure: Bush Key
description: Visitor access to the north side of Bush Key is suspended through Fall 2026 to protect nesting Sooty Terns, Brown Noddies, and Magnificent Frigatebirds. The south (harbor) side remains open. Boaters must maintain a distance of 100 feet from the island's mean low tide.

title: Rehabilitation of Colonial Parkway Continues
description: Construction on the Colonial Parkway is affecting travel in many sections. Please follow all detour and caution signs. Additional information can be found at the link below.

title: Old Springs Trail near Tennessee Valley closed starting March 23, 2026
description: The trail will be closed for boardwalk replacement and general maintenance until early August. Surrounding trails will remain open to provide an alternative route. Please pay attention to safety signage and stay out of the closed area.

title: BE AWARE OF HIGH-WATER DANGER
description: Visitors should be aware of high-water danger. Heavy rain has made crossing flowing water hazardous for hikers and horseback riders.

title: Elevated Fire Risk
description: Fire danger is especially high in southeastern Arizona March through June. Red Flag Warnings and fire restrictions may be implemented without notice. Campers: be prepared to go without a campfire and to cook using an enclosed propane device. Be cautious at all times and use water to drown fires. Note: Ground fires and wood collection are prohibited at all times. Stand up grills are available only for overnight campers.

title: Flooding and Landslides Affecting Park Complex
description: The park complex experienced severe weather events following flood damage in December 2025, causing unsafe conditions and limiting basic infrastructure in some areas. Some services are available in Stehekin with winter and spring operations.

title: Large vehicle restrictions come into effect on June 7, 2026
description: Beginning June 7, 2026, the park will enforce existing size and weight limits on the Zion-Mt. Carmel Highway. Vehicles that exceed length, width, height, or weight limits will not be allowed to travel between Canyon Junction and the park's East Entrance. See the upcoming limits on the page linked below.

title: Bacteria Levels at Arizona Hot Springs
description: Bacterial levels in water samples taken from Arizona Hot Springs exceed Local, State and National Park Service standards for recreational activity. Due to the increased human health risk, the National Park Service warns to avoid contact with these waters until further notice. Please contact the Park at 702-293-8990 for updates on this advisory.

park_name: Puʻuhonua o Hōnaunau National Historical Park
title: Annual and lifetime passes not available for purchase
description: Annual and lifetime passes are not currently available for purchase at Puʻuhonua o Hōnaunau National Historical Park. As an alternative, you can purchase an annual pass online.

title: Invasive Vegetation Removal
description: Watch for crews treating invasive plants along Snake Bight Trail, Rowdy Bend Road, and other Flamingo areas. Approved herbicides are being used. Stay alert for workers, equipment, and posted signs.

title: High Levels of Visitation
description: The park will be experiencing high levels of visitation from March through May. Expect limited parking, long lines, and crowded areas. Please have a credit or debit card ready or valid park pass with ID when checking in at the visitor center.

title: High Levels of Visitation
description: The park will be experiencing high levels of visitation from March through May. Expect limited parking, long lines, and crowded areas. Please have a credit or debit card ready or valid park pass with ID when checking in at the visitor center. Parking or stopping is not permitted on the roads outside the park gate at any time.

park_name: Chesapeake & Ohio Canal National Historical Park
title: DC Water Sewer Overflow
description: DC Water is managing a sanitary sewer overflow from the Potomac Interceptor near Lock 10. Anyone with questions or in need of support can reach out to DC Water’s 24-Hour Command Center at (202) 612-3400 or visit https://www.dcwater.com/about-dc-water/media/potomac-interceptor-collapse.

title: K-177 Highway Closed from Council Grove South to the Preserve, Use Detour
description: Beginning Monday, March 2, K-177 will close for a bridge replacement from Council Grove to the U.S. 50/K-177 Strong City intersection. Tallgrass Prairie National Preserve will remain open but must be accessed from the south or follow the detour west out of Council Grove. K-177 thru traffic will be directed to follow the state detour on U.S. 56, U.S. 56/U.S. 77 and K-150. KDOT uses state highways for official detour routes for safety, weight and maintenance concerns. Go to KDOT website for detour map.

title: Critical Backcountry Updates/Closures
description: Visit this link for the inner canyon weather forecast, current trail closures, today's heat risk, and drinking water availability.

title: Visitor Center Restrooms Closed for Renovation
description: The restrooms at the Kris Eggle Visitor Center will be closed from the end of February until April for renovation. Temporary restrooms will be available in the visitor center parking lot. Visitors in need of more permanent facilities are advised to use those at the Twin Peaks Campground.

park_name: Charles Young Buffalo Soldiers National Monument
title: Visitor Center Hours of Operation
description: The Visitor Center is open Friday - Sunday from 9:00am to 4:00pm

title: Several Trails Close For Peregrine Falcon Nesting Season
description: Effective March 1, areas including Jordan Cliffs Trail, Penobscot East Trail, Precipice Trail, and Valley Cove Trail are closed to protect nesting peregrine falcons. The parking lot for the Precipice Trail is also closed. Rangers strictly enforce closures.

title: Lehman Caves Closure
description: Lehman Caves and the Lehman Caves Visitor Center are closed to the public as work begins on a six-month project to replace the cave’s 48-year-old, failing electric lights. Project completion is planned for summer 2026. The rest of Great Basin National Park remains open.

title: Park Auditorium Closed Until Further Notice
description: Due to some damage from recent storms, the auditorium will be closed until further notice. We are sorry for the inconvenience.

title: The Painted Desert Oasis Gift Shop, Gas Station and Restaurant are Open!
description: The Painted Desert Oasis gift shop, gas station and restaurant located at the north entrance of the park are open, and restrooms are available during construction. Visitor center operations are currently located at the Painted Desert Inn, two miles past the entrance station.

title: Fredericksburg Battlefield Visitor Center Closed for Renovations
description: Starting November 20, 2025 the Fredericksburg Battlefield Visitor Center will be closed while updates are made to building systems. Front Desk operations including visitor orientation will move to the adjacent Fredericksburg Battlefield Bookstore. The Chancellorsville Visitor Center remains open to visitors 9am - 5pm all week.

title: All Trails on Shiloh Battlefield Closed
description: Due to tree and limb hazards from the recent ice storm, all trails on the battlefield are considered closed. Cleanup operations have begun on the park. Please be aware of workers and equipment on the roadways.

title: March 2: Fishing, ORV, Boat Ramp, Archery Passes on Sale at Recreation.gov
description: On March 2, 2026, at 10am, the following passes will be available for purchase ONLINE ONLY through Recreation.gov: Fishing Access (Parkwide), Off-Road Vehicle (Breezy Point), Boat Ramp Parking (Great Kills Park) and Archery Parking (Floyd Bennett Field). Sale Period March 2-March 31. All vehicle decals MUST be picked up by April 12.

title: Parking impacted due to the Legacy Restoration Fund Project
description: The Lower Farm parking lot near the Mule Barn is closed due to the Legacy Restoration Fund Project. The Upper parking lot located near the Orangery has reduced capacity due to a partial closure because of the Legacy Restoration Fund Project. To learn more about parking at Hampton NHS, click the link or visit the alerts page and then scroll down to the area that talks about parking.

title: Glenmont Mansion and Grounds Closed
description: Glenmont, the Edison family home and surrounding estate grounds, are currently closed until further notice due to operational and safety considerations. The Laboratory Complex remains open Thursday–Sunday, 10 a.m.–4 p.m. Please check this page for updates.

title: Park Headquarters Phones are Down
description: Phones at park headquarters are currently down. Please call the visitor center at Painted Desert Inn 928-235-2467 or the Visitor Services office 928-235-2391 between 8 am and 5 pm. You can also send us an email at pefo_information@nps.gov.

title: Illinois Memorial Closed for Restoration
description: The memorial itself closed for restoration starting on September 16, 2025. Starting on March 2, 2026, access to Tour Stops 2 and 3 will be temporarily closed. The closure point will be at the Shirley House. Visitors will still be able to continue north along Union Avenue and access the remainder of the Tour Road. This section will remain closed for the duration of the Illinois Memorial Restoration. The project is expected to be completed by September 2026.

title: Verona Segment — Badger Prairie Park Construction
description: Construction at Badger Prairie Park may mean fencing and a small Trail detour west of the parking area. Restrooms and shelters may be closed. The Dispersed Camping Area is available but may have no restroom or water availability. The Trail is open. Stay alert and follow posted detour signs.

title: No Horse-Riding or Pack Animal Permits Issued Until Further Notice
description: Horse-riding and pack animal permits will not be issued until further notice. Horses and pack animals should not be brought into the park until permits resume.

title: Condition of Turkey Creek Trail
description: The South Turkey Creek Trail, located near the Harbor Bay Campground Area, is currently open for hiking for the first mile; however, the remainder of the trail is underwater and cannot be safely accessed.

title: Heavy Traffic and High Visitation Alert
description: Pinnacles experiences extremely high visitation on weekends, holidays, and during favorable weather, especially in the spring. Expect long lines and delays between 10:00 am and 3:00 pm. If you're planning to hike the High Peaks, Condor Gulch, Balconies, or Moses Spring Trail, it’s recommended to arrive before 8:00 am (parking may fill earlier). Additionally, campground access maybe temporarily delayed during peak periods of visitation when ALL incoming traffic is held.

title: Temporary Closure of Right Lane of Clara Barton Parkway
description: Temporary Closure of the Clara Barton Parkway Right Lane at Site 17, wooded areas between C&O Canal and Lock 10 parking lot, north to Lock 14, required to address ongoing safety concerns from DC Water emergency sanitary sewer overflow & bypass and pipe repair operations. Click on "More" below for specifica details on the closure.

title: Road workers on Park Road in Davis Bayou
description: Phase II of the Davis Bayou Bicycle and Pedestrian Path is in process. Visitors may encounter minor traffic delays and flaggers on Eagle Point and Park Road.

title: Trail closures possible with winter weather
description: Portions of trails may close when snow or ice are present. Please check in at the visitor center for current trail conditions.

title: Ice caves are CLOSED due to unstable ice conditions.
description: The ice shelf near Meyers Beach at Apostle Islands National Lakeshore was broken up during a strong winter storm that impacted the region beginning on 2/17. Access to the ice caves within the National Lakeshore is closed to visitation.

park_name: Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park
title: Overlook Park Lot Unavailable After Dark
description: The park parking lot at 72 McBride Extension is open from dawn to dusk every day. Any vehicles left in the parking lot after dusk (i.e. sunset) are subject to towing at owner's expense.

park_name: Great Sand Dunes National Park & Preserve
title: Speed Limits are Much Lower Inside Park than Surrounding Highways
description: Speed limits for the park include 30 MPH near the park boundary and Great Sand Dunes Sign, and as low as 10 MPH near the Headquarters and Entrance Station. Speed limits are in place to protect park visitors, pedestrians, and wildlife in the park. Please drive carefully and responsibly.

park_name: Lyndon B Johnson National Historical Park
title: Park Bookstore Has Gone Cashless
description: We are excited to announce that our park store, operated by Western National Parks, is now operating on a cashless basis. For your convenience, they accept all major credit and debit cards, as well as mobile payment options.

title: Buildings located on the farm side are closed
description: Buildings located on the farm side of Hampton National Historic Site are closed due to construction related to the ongoing Legacy Restoration Fund Project. Visit the project webpage for more information or click the link to learn more.

title: Angels Landing Pilot Permit Program
description: Everyone who hikes Angels Landing needs to have a permit. Learn why you need a permit and how to get one. This requirement does not affect entering the park or riding the park shuttle (when it is running).

title: RV and trailer precautions: be aware of vehicle restrictions before visit.
description: As the busy season ramps up, traffic will be denser and can result in delays and complications particularly for owners of longer vehicles. The Tucson Mountain District (west side) of the park can accommodate vehicles up to 40ft in total length on the loop drive and has RV parking available at the Visitor Center, and the Rincon Mountain District (east side) can accommodate vehicles up to 40ft in length on the loop drive but has no RV parking available at the Visitor Center.

title: No Water at Hosmer Campground
description: As of September 30, 2025, water at Hosmer Campground in the Summit District is shutoff due to a waterline break. Water is available 24-hours at Headquarters Visitor Center one mile up the road. At this point we don't have an estimate for when repairs will be complete.

title: 12/12/2025 Update - Parkway and Campground closed from MP 181-204.
